```{python}
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from matplotlib import cm
from matplotlib.colors import Normalize, to_hex, LinearSegmentedColormap
import numpy as np
import os
import sys
from pathlib import Path
from IPython.display import display, Markdown, Latex

# --- CONFIGURATION ---
if is_html:
    try:
        from itables import init_notebook_mode, options
        init_notebook_mode(all_interactive=True)
        options.allow_html = True
        options.maxColumns = 0        
        options.scrollX = True        
        options.dom = '<"top-row"lf>rt<"bottom-row"p>i'      
        options.pageLength = -1       
        options.classes = ["display", "nowrap", "cell-border", "compact"]
        options.style = "width:100%; margin:auto;"
    except ImportError:
        pass
else:
    # --- PDF SETTINGS ---
    pd.options.display.notebook_repr_html = False 
    pd.options.display.max_rows = None
    pd.options.display.max_columns = None
    pd.options.display.max_colwidth = None

# --- GRAPH SETTINGS ---
plt.rcParams['figure.figsize'] = (10, 3.2) 
plt.rcParams['figure.autolayout'] = True

# --- HELPER FUNCTIONS ---
def smart_display(df, styler=None):
    if df is not None:
        if is_html:
            if styler is not None:
                display(styler)
            else:
                display(df)
        else:
            if len(df.columns) > 10:
                font_size = r"\tiny"
            elif len(df.columns) > 7:
                font_size = r"\footnotesize"
            else:
                font_size = r"\normalsize"
            print(font_size)

            df = latex_escape_df(df)
            
            latex_string = df.to_latex(
                escape=False, # Needed for some latex commands
                index=False, 
                longtable=True,
            )

            adjusted_latex = latex_string.replace(
                r'\begin{tabular}',
                r'\begin{adjustbox}{max width=\textwidth}\begin{tabular}'
            ).replace(
                r'\end{tabular}',
                r'\end{tabular}\end{adjustbox}'
            )
            print('\n' + adjusted_latex + '\n')
            print(r"\normalsize")

def display_split_tables(df):
    """
    Splits tables with empty rows to multiple
    mini tables
    """
    if df is None or df.empty:
        return

    # If the first row of headers are just strings of numbers, we drop them
    if all(str(col).isdigit() for col in df.columns):
        df.columns = range(len(df.columns)) 
        
    is_empty = df.apply(lambda x: x.astype(str).str.strip().replace('nan', '').eq('').all(), axis=1)
    
    # Group consecutive non-empty rows into single blocks
    blocks = []
    current_block = []
    
    for i, row_is_empty in enumerate(is_empty):
        if not row_is_empty:
            current_block.append(df.iloc[i])
        else:
            if current_block:
                blocks.append(pd.DataFrame(current_block))
                current_block = []
    if current_block: # Catch the last block
        blocks.append(pd.DataFrame(current_block))

    for block in blocks:
        block = block.replace('', np.nan).dropna(axis=1, how='all').fillna('')
        
        if block.empty or len(block.columns) == 0:
            continue

        # Check for Title Rows
        row_content = [str(c).strip() for c in block.iloc[0] if str(c).strip() and str(c).strip() != 'nan']
        
        if len(block) == 1 and len(row_content) == 1:
            display(Markdown(f"#### {row_content[0]}"))
        else:
            # Display as a single cohesive table
            smart_display(block)
        
        display(Markdown("<br>"))

def _rgb(val, vmin, vmax):
    r, g, b, _ = _CMAP(Normalize(vmin, vmax, clip=True)(val))
    return r, g, b

def gradient_style(df, cols, html=True):
    vmin = df[cols].min().min()
    vmax = df[cols].max().max()
    norm = Normalize(vmin, vmax, clip=True)

    if html:
        def css(val):
            r, g, b, _ = _CMAP(norm(val))
            return f"background-color: {to_hex((r, g, b))}"

        return (
            df.style
              .apply(lambda s: [css(v) for v in s], subset=cols)
              .format(precision=2)
        )

    # PDF path
    df_pdf = df.copy()
    for col in cols:
        df_pdf[col] = df_pdf[col].apply(
            lambda v: (
                f"\\cellcolor[rgb]{{"
                f"{_CMAP(norm(v))[0]:.3f},"
                f"{_CMAP(norm(v))[1]:.3f},"
                f"{_CMAP(norm(v))[2]:.3f}}}"
                f"{v:.2f}"
            )
        )
    return df_pdf

def latex_escape_df(df):
    df = df.copy()

    # Escape column names
    df.columns = [
        c.replace("\\", r"\textbackslash{}")
         .replace("_", r"\_")
         .replace("%", r"\%")
         .replace("&", r"\&")
         .replace("#", r"\#")
         .replace("$", r"\$")
        if isinstance(c, str) else c
        for c in df.columns
    ]

    # Escape string cell values
    for col in df.select_dtypes(include="object").columns:
        if df[col].astype(str).str.contains("cellcolor").any():
            continue

        df[col] = (
            df[col]
            .str.replace("\\", r"\textbackslash{}", regex=False)
            .str.replace("_", r"\_", regex=False)
            .str.replace("%", r"\%", regex=False)
            .str.replace("&", r"\&", regex=False)
            .str.replace("#", r"\#", regex=False)
            .str.replace("$", r"\$", regex=False)
        )

    return df


def load_data(filename):
    path = curr_dir / filename
    if path.exists():
        return pd.read_csv(path)
    return None

def annotate_delta(x_pos, prev, curr):
    delta = curr - prev
    # Calculate percentage change and handle potential division by zero
    percent = (delta / prev * 100) if prev != 0 else 0
    arrow = "▲" if delta >= 0 else "▼"
    
    # Display the absolute change and the percentage growth/shrinkage 
    plt.text(
        x_pos,
        max(prev, curr) * 1.05,
        f"{arrow} {abs(delta):.2f} GB\n({percent:+.1f}%)",
        ha="center",
        fontweight="bold",
        fontsize=9
    )

def calc_growth(inst, mode="physical"):
    curr = db_data[inst][f'cur_{mode}_size'].iloc[0, 0]
    prev = db_data[inst][f'prev_{mode}_size'].iloc[0, 0]
    pct = ((curr - prev) / prev * 100) if prev != 0 else 0
    return f"{abs(pct):.2f}% {'Increase' if pct >= 0 else 'Decrease'}"

# --- DATA INITIALIZATION ---
csv_dir = Path("../dbhc_csv")
csv_subdirs = sorted([p for p in csv_dir.iterdir() if p.is_dir()])
curr_dir = csv_subdirs[-1]
instances = ["bancsarc", "bancsdb", "bancsrep"]
servers = ["DR", "NODE1", "NODE2"]
sections = ["DB SIZE", "TABLESPACE", "BACKUP", "DATA GUARD", "LOG SWITCH", "ASM", "FS", "CRS", "LISTENER"]
tablespace_threshold = 80
asm_threshold = 80
fs_threshold = 70
db_data = {}
# Controls color formatting in styled tables
_CMAP = LinearSegmentedColormap.from_list(
    "pastel_green_yellow_red",
    ["#b9f8beff", "#fff7a3ff", "#ffb3abff"]  # light green → cream → light red
)

# Database/Instance Details
for inst in instances:
    db_data[inst] = {
        "backup": load_data(f"backup_{inst}.csv"),
        "control": load_data(f"controlfile_{inst}.csv"),
        "cur_physical_size": load_data(f"db_size_physical_{inst}_cur.csv"),
        "cur_logical_size": load_data(f"db_size_logical_{inst}_cur.csv"),
        "data_guard": load_data(f"data_guard_{inst}.csv"),
        "invalid_objects": load_data(f"invalid_objects_{inst}.csv"),
        "prev_physical_size": load_data(f"db_size_physical_{inst}_prev.csv"),
        "prev_logical_size": load_data(f"db_size_logical_{inst}_prev.csv"),
        "sync": load_data(f"data_guard_{inst}.csv"),
        "tablespace": load_data(f"tablespace_{inst}.csv"),
    }

# Server Details
for server in servers:
    db_data[server] = {
        "fs": load_data(f"fs_util_{server}.csv"),
        "lstnr": load_data(f"lstnr_{server}.csv"),
    }
    if server != "NODE2":
        db_data[server]["crs"] = load_data(f"crs_{server}.csv")

# AWR Details
for server in servers:
    for inst in instances:
        if server != "DR":
            db_data[f"{server}_{inst}"] = { 
                "awrrpt": load_data(f"awrrpt_{server}_{inst}.csv")
            }
```

# Executive Summary

::: {.panel-tabset}
```{python}
#| output: asis
for sec in sections:
    display(Markdown(f"## {sec.upper()}"))

    match sec:
        case "DB SIZE":
            for inst in instances:
                phys = calc_growth(inst, "physical")
                log = calc_growth(inst, "logical")
                display(Markdown(
                    f"- **{inst.upper()}**:\n"
                    f"    - **{phys}** Physical Size vs Previous Month\n"
                    f"    - **{log}** Logical Size vs Previous Month"
                    ))
        case "TABLESPACE":
            for inst in instances:
                try:
                    df = db_data[inst]["tablespace"]
                    tablespace_names = df.columns[0]
                    usage_pct = df.columns[-1]
                    results = df.loc[df[usage_pct] > tablespace_threshold, tablespace_names]

                    if len(results) > 0:
                        tablespace_status = f"{", ".join(results)} is/are above {tablespace_threshold}% usage"
                    else:
                        tablespace_status = "Tablespaces are at a good level"
                except Exception:
                    tablespace_status = "No tablespaces detected"
                display(Markdown(f"- **{inst.upper()}**: {tablespace_status}."))
        case "BACKUP":
            for inst in instances:
                try:
                    df = db_data[inst]["backup"]
                    backup_status = df.columns[-4]
                    backup_start_time = df.columns[0]
                    results = df.loc[df[backup_status] != "COMPLETED", backup_start_time]

                    if len(results) > 0:
                        backup_status = f"Backups started on {", ".join(results)} is/are incomplete"
                    else:
                        backup_status = "All backups on report are completed"
                except Exception:
                    backup_status = "No backups detected"
                display(Markdown(f"- **{inst.upper()}**: {backup_status}."))
        case "DATA GUARD":
            for inst in instances:
                try:
                    df = db_data[inst]["data_guard"]
                    thread_num = df.columns[1]
                    primary_seq = df.columns[2]
                    standby_seq = df.columns[3]
                    results = df.loc[df[primary_seq] != df[standby_seq], thread_num]

                    if len(results) > 0:
                        dg_status = f"Primary and standby in thread/s {", ".join(results)} is/are out-of-sync"
                    else:
                        dg_status = "Primary and standby are in-sync"
                except Exception:
                    dg_status = "No data guards detected"
                display(Markdown(f"- **{inst.upper()}**: {dg_status}."))
        case "LOG SWITCH":
            for inst in instances:
                log_switch_status = "test"
                display(Markdown(f"- **{inst.upper()}**: {log_switch_status}."))
        case "ASM":
            for inst in instances:
                try:
                    df = pd.read_csv(curr_dir / "asm.csv")
                    asm_name = df.columns[0]
                    usage_pct = df.columns[-1]
                    results = df.loc[df[usage_pct] > asm_threshold, asm_name]

                    if len(results) > 0:
                        asm_status = f"Diskgroup {", ".join(results)} is/are above {asm_threshold}% usage"
                    else:
                        asm_status = f"All diskgroups are below {asm_threshold}% usage"
                except Exception:
                    asm_status = "No ASM detected"
                display(Markdown(f"- **{inst.upper()}**: {asm_status}."))
        case "FS":
            for server in servers:
                try:
                    df = db_data[server]["fs"]
                    fs_name = df.columns[0]
                    usage_pct = df.columns[-2]
                    results = df.loc[df[usage_pct].str.strip('%').astype(int) > fs_threshold, fs_name]

                    if len(results) > 0:
                        fs_status = f"Filesystem {", ".join(results)} is/are above {fs_threshold}% usage"
                    else:
                        fs_status = f"All mounts are below {fs_threshold}% usage"
                except Exception:
                    fs_status = "No filesystems detected"
                display(Markdown(f"- **{server.upper()}**: {fs_status}."))
        case "CRS":
            for inst in instances:
                print("test")
        case "LISTENER":
            for inst in instances:
                print("test")
    
    display(Markdown(f" "))
```
:::

# Database Report

## Database Size Status

::: {.panel-tabset}
```{python}
#| output: asis
for inst in instances:
    display(Markdown(f"### {inst.upper()}"))
    
    prev_phys = db_data[inst]['prev_physical_size'].iloc[0, 0]
    cur_phys  = db_data[inst]['cur_physical_size'].iloc[0, 0]
    prev_log  = db_data[inst]['prev_logical_size'].iloc[0, 0]
    cur_log   = db_data[inst]['cur_logical_size'].iloc[0, 0]

    labels = ["Physical Size", "Logical Size"]
    prev_vals = [prev_phys, prev_log]
    curr_vals = [cur_phys, cur_log]
    x = np.arange(len(labels))
    width = 0.35
    
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7.5, 4.5))
    ax.bar(x - width/2, prev_vals, width, label="Previous", color="#3498db")
    ax.bar(x + width/2, curr_vals, width, label="Current", color="#2ecc71")

    for i, v in enumerate(prev_vals):
        ax.text(i - width/2, v, f"{v:.1f}G", ha="center", va="bottom")
    for i, v in enumerate(curr_vals):
        ax.text(i + width/2, v, f"{v:.1f}G", ha="center", va="bottom")

    annotate_delta(x[0], prev_phys, cur_phys)
    annotate_delta(x[1], prev_log, cur_log)

    ax.set_ylabel("Size (GB)")
    ax.set_title(f"Database Size Analysis: {inst.upper()}")
    ax.set_xticks(x, labels)
    ax.legend()
    
    ax.set_ylim(0, max(prev_vals + curr_vals) * 1.4) 
    
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()
    
    display(Markdown(f" "))
```
:::

::: {.callout-note}
## Findings and Recommendations
This is the content of the Data Size Report
:::

## Tablespace Status

::: {.panel-tabset}
```{python}
#| output: asis

for inst in instances:
    display(Markdown(f"### {inst.upper()}"))
    if db_data[inst]['tablespace'] is not None:
        df = db_data[inst]['tablespace']
        usage_pct = df.columns[-1]
        styled_df = gradient_style(df, cols=[usage_pct], html=is_html)
        if is_html:
            smart_display(df, styler=styled_df)
        else:
            smart_display(styled_df)
    display(Markdown(f" "))
```
:::

::: {.callout-tip}
## Findings and Recommendations
This is the content of the Table Size Report
:::

## Control File Status

::: {.panel-tabset}
```{python}
#| output: asis

for inst in instances:
    display(Markdown(f"### {inst.upper()}"))
    if db_data[inst]['control'] is not None:
        df = db_data[inst]['control']
        smart_display(df)
    display(Markdown(f" "))
```
:::

::: {.callout-warning}
## Findings and Recommendations
This is the content of the Control File Report
:::

## RMAN Backup Status

::: {.panel-tabset}
```{python}
#| output: asis

for inst in instances:
    display(Markdown(f"### {inst.upper()}"))
    if db_data[inst]['backup'] is not None:
        df = db_data[inst]['backup']
        smart_display(df)
    display(Markdown(f" "))
```
:::

::: {.callout-caution}
## Findings and Recommendations
This is the content of the RMAN Backup Report
:::

## Data Guard Status

::: {.panel-tabset}
```{python}
#| output: asis

for inst in instances:
    display(Markdown(f"### {inst.upper()}"))
    if db_data[inst]['data_guard'] is not None:
        df = db_data[inst]['data_guard']
        smart_display(df)
    display(Markdown(f" "))
```
:::

::: {.callout-important}
## Findings and Recommendations
This is the content of the Data Guard Report
:::

## Invalid Object Status

::: {.panel-tabset}
```{python}
#| output: asis

for inst in instances:
    display(Markdown(f"### {inst.upper()}"))
    if db_data[inst]['invalid_objects'] is not None:
        df = db_data[inst]['invalid_objects']
        smart_display(df)
    display(Markdown(f" "))
```
:::

::: {.callout-important}
## Findings and Recommendations
This is the content of the Invalid Objects Report
:::

## Log Switch Status

::: {.panel-tabset}
```{python}
#| output: asis

for inst in instances:
    display(Markdown(f"### {inst.upper()}"))
    if inst == "bancsarc":
        df = pd.read_csv(curr_dir / "archiving_instance_archivelogswitch_19c.csv")
    elif inst == "bancsdb":
        df = pd.read_csv(curr_dir / "operating_instance_archivelogswitch_19c.csv")
    elif inst == "bancsrep":
        df = pd.read_csv(curr_dir / "reporting_instance_archivelogswitch_19c.csv")
    else:
        df = None
    if df is not None:
        log_cols = df.columns[2:] 
        styled_df = gradient_style(df, cols=log_cols, html=is_html)
        if is_html:
            smart_display(df, styler=styled_df)
        else:
            smart_display(styled_df)
    display(Markdown(f" "))
```
:::

::: {.callout-important}
## Findings and Recommendations
This is the content of the Log Switch Status Report
:::

# Server Details Report

## ASM Storage Status

```{python}
#| output: asis
df = pd.read_csv(curr_dir / "asm.csv")
if df is not None:
    smart_display(df)
```

::: {.callout-important}
## Findings and Recommendations
This is the content of the ASM Report
:::

## File System Utilization Status

::: {.panel-tabset}
```{python}
#| output: asis
for server in servers:
    display(Markdown(f"### {server.upper()}"))
    if db_data[server]['fs'] is not None:
        df = db_data[server]['fs']
        smart_display(df)
    display(Markdown(f" "))
```
:::

::: {.callout-important}
## Findings and Recommendations
This is the content of the FS Utilization Report
:::

## Cluster Resource Status
::: {.panel-tabset}
```{python}
#| output: asis
for server in servers:
    if server != "NODE2":
        display(Markdown(f"### {server.upper()}"))
        if db_data[server]['crs'] is not None:
            df = db_data[server]['crs']
            smart_display(df)
        display(Markdown(f" "))
```
:::

::: {.callout-important}
## Findings and Recommendations
This is the content of the CRS Report
:::

## Listener Status
::: {.panel-tabset}
```{python}
#| output: asis
for server in servers:
    display(Markdown(f"### {server.upper()}"))
    if db_data[server]['lstnr'] is not None:
        df = db_data[server]['lstnr']
        smart_display(df)
    display(Markdown(f" "))
```
:::

::: {.callout-important}
## Findings and Recommendations
This is the content of the Listener Status Report
:::

# AWR Report 

## NODE 1 AWR Report
::: {.panel-tabset}
```{python}
#| output: asis
for inst in instances:
    display(Markdown(f"### {inst.upper()}"))
    if db_data[f"NODE1_{inst}"]['awrrpt'] is not None:
        df = db_data[f"NODE1_{inst}"]['awrrpt']
        display_split_tables(df)
    display(Markdown(f" "))
```
:::

::: {.callout-important}
## Findings and Recommendations
This is the content of the AWR Report for Node 1
:::


## NODE 2 AWR Report
::: {.panel-tabset}
```{python}
#| output: asis
for inst in instances:
    display(Markdown(f"### {inst.upper()}"))
    if db_data[f"NODE2_{inst}"]['awrrpt'] is not None:
        df = db_data[f"NODE2_{inst}"]['awrrpt']
        display_split_tables(df)
    display(Markdown(f" "))
```
:::

::: {.callout-important}
## Findings and Recommendations
This is the content of the AWR Report for Node 2
:::
